3	This file is read by Claude at the start of every session. It describes how to work in this
4	repo — the skills available, the canonical workflow, and behavioral rules accumulated from
5	past mistakes. It does not repeat what is in Readme.md or the individual skill files.

7	---
8

10	
11	Before starting any task, check whether an AGENTS.md exists in the directory you're working
12	in or any of its parent directories (up to this root). If one exists, read it — it contains
13	context specific to that part of the codebase. Known nested files:
14	
15	<!-- nested-agents-index -->
16	<!-- Add entries here as nested AGENTS.md files are created, e.g.:             -->
17	<!-- - [skills/plan/AGENTS.md](skills/plan/AGENTS.md) — plan skill conventions -->
18	<!-- nested-agents-index-end -->
19	
20	If you create work in a subdirectory that would benefit from persistent local conventions,
21	create an AGENTS.md there and link it in the index above.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Canonical Workflow
26	
27	For any non-trivial feature, work proceeds in this order. Do not skip steps — each one is a
28	skill that can be invoked explicitly.
29	
30	```
31	/problem-spec   →  define the problem, produce docs/<feature>/spec.md
32	/plan           →  break into TDD chunks, produce docs/<feature>/plan.md + chunk sub-plans
33	                   (requires spec.md to exist)
34	
35	  For each chunk:
36	    write tests (red) → write code (green) → refactor → commit
37	    /verify     →  E2E check against live system; produces docs/verify/<branch>-<date>.md
38	    /simplify   →  clean up staged code; produces docs/simplify/<branch>-<date>.md
39	    /review     →  correctness check; produces docs/reviews/<branch>-<date>.md
40	    human       →  developer signs off
41	
42	/learn-from-mistakes  →  log corrections and gaps; updates .claude/learnings.md
43	```
44	
45	Failures in `/verify`, `/simplify`, or `/review` require fixes or a plan update before
46	proceeding. The Human column in the plan dashboard cannot be marked ✅ while any prior
47	column is ❌.
48	
49	---

51	## Skills Reference
52	
53	Each skill has a full SKILL.md. This table is a quick reference only — read the SKILL.md
54	for complete instructions.
55	
56	| Skill | Invoke | Purpose |
57	|---|---|---|
58	| problem-spec | `/problem-spec` | Define what is and isn't being solved; produce a spec doc |
59	| plan | `/plan` | Break a spec into testable TDD chunks with a status dashboard |
60	| verify | `/verify` | E2E verification — real curl or browser automation against a live system |
61	| simplify | `/simplify` | Apply clear improvements; suggest uncertain ones; never touch tests |
62	| review | `/review` | Find bugs, missed edge cases, unhandled errors; report only |
63	| learn-from-mistakes | `/learn-from-mistakes` | Log corrections and gaps after human sign-off |
64	
65	Skills live in `skills/`. Each skill directory contains a `SKILL.md` and optionally
66	`sub-skills/`, `evals/`, and supporting scripts.
67	
68	---

72	Rules added here by `/learn-from-mistakes` when a pattern occurs 3+ times. Starts empty.
73	
74	<!-- learned-rules -->
75	<!-- learned-rules-end -->
76

80	
81	- Not a duplicate of Readme.md (see that for repo purpose)
82	- Not a directory listing (derive structure from the repo itself)
83	- Not a copy of individual SKILL.md files (read those directly)

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing.
